result.summary: Customer.io exposes three documented HTTP APIs (Track, App, and Pipelines), an MCP server for AI tools, and a `cio` CLI.
result.description: Customer.io is a customer engagement platform for sending automated, data-driven messages across channels like email, SMS, push, in-app, WhatsApp, and webhooks. It also includes customer data pipeline capabilities and AI features for working with campaigns and workspace data.

track_api_key.label: Track API site ID and API key
track_api_key.setup: In Customer.io, go to [Account Settings → API Credentials](https://docs.customer.io/accounts/settings/managing-credentials/). Create or copy your Track API credentials: a `site ID` and a `Track API key`. These are used together for Track API authentication.

app_api_key.label: App API key
app_api_key.setup: In Customer.io, go to [Account Settings → API Credentials](https://docs.customer.io/accounts/settings/managing-credentials/). Create or copy an `App API key`. Customer.io docs note that the App API uses bearer authorization, and transactional API setup pages direct you to create the App API key there.

cio_cli_login.label: Customer.io CLI login session
cio_cli_login.setup: Install the CLI following [Get started with the Customer.io CLI](https://docs.customer.io/ai/cli/get-started/), then authenticate by running the CLI login flow as documented there. This creates a local Customer.io-authenticated CLI session for the `cio` command.
